plugins.name: zaksak
plugins.description: A collection of Claude Code utilities by Zak Sakata

plugins.name: spec-ops
plugins.description: Spec workflow (write → refine → launch → verify): write-spec is the workflow entrypoint that turns a bare idea into a spec — eliciting and distilling requirements (full rigor) before drafting concise feature specs at a requested rigor (light/standard a code-free, implementation-agnostic WHAT → full deep spec that may pin implementation) that open with a flat, id'd Acceptance Criteria contract (optionally organized into ordered named groups); refine-spec hardens a draft into an implementation-ready spec, commits the grounded group order, and hunts unstated constraints; launch-spec compiles the ready spec into one of three drivers (/goal, ultracode, /batch), each completed by a verify-spec gate on every acceptance criterion (emit-only), phasing a large or hard-sequenced build by AC group; verify-spec checks the implementation matches the spec's claims and every acceptance criterion against real code, git, and live state — with the evidence standard scaled to each criterion and a verification method recorded per AC — and flags delivered code that maps to no criterion (backward coverage), plus a report-only spec-linkage hygiene sweep (a deterministic detector for the greppable patterns + a judgment pass) that flags delivered code/docs/tests still referencing the build spec (AC-ids, build-phase numbers, spec ids, predecessor provenance, spec/history-named identifiers, inert background context) so the shipped artifact reads standalone; on a spec re-run it drifts against the last clean verification — re-grounding only criteria whose evidence moved and flagging regressions. spec-ops also commits the spec artifact at each stage — write-spec the draft, refine-spec the implementation-ready spec (Stop-hook-enforced), launch-spec bakes a per-phase commit cadence into the /goal driver — each commit scoped to the spec file (never a broad add) and never pushed. A verify→refine handoff carries verify-spec's backward-sweep proposed ACs to refine-spec (via /tmp, written by the verify Stop hook), so a missed-requirement finding amends the spec on the next refine run without re-keying — verify stays read-only.

plugins.name: ralph
plugins.description: Ralph Loop — autonomous agentic coding workflow with sandbox isolation, scoped memory, and self-managing task iteration

plugins.name: skill-manager
plugins.description: Manage Claude Code skills from inside any project: configure which plugins a project uses, publish/sync local skills to a central marketplace, remove them, and diagnose and auto-repair the setup. Adapts to any repo layout; authoring is delegated to the native skill-creator skill.

plugins.name: worktree-ops
plugins.description: Git worktree workflow for parallel Claude Code sessions, built on native worktree support, with deterministic enforcement (a skill-scoped PreToolUse guard, active only while the pull/merge skills run, blocks squash merges and committing unresolved conflicts; helper scripts make pre-flight, conflict listing, and merge-confirm repeatable): create-worktree spins up an isolated worktree and flips the session into it (fresh from origin, .worktreeinclude env files copied, optional issue/PR seed, idempotently keeps .claude/worktrees/ gitignored, opt-in setup script); list-worktrees shows a dashboard of every worktree (ahead/behind, dirty, PR, current marked); pull-worktree rebases/merges a worktree onto a chosen base branch with an ask-first conflict policy; merge-worktree commits, pushes, opens/merges the PR into a chosen target (never squashed) only after confirming MERGED, runs an opt-in teardown script, then removes the worktree and returns to main.

plugins.name: gh-projects
plugins.description: Run a small team's entire software lifecycle on GitHub Projects v2 — deterministic, free, and GitHub-native. scaffold-repo copies a golden-template Project (fields, 8 views, 9 Insights charts) across the org and installs the repo automation (issue forms, PR template, board-sync + signals-sync workflows, the self-contained board-status deploy action, release notes, CODEOWNERS, no-squash merge setting) plus per-repo auto-add (actions/add-to-project, SHA-pinned, App-token), links repos + a team to the Project; route-issue projects an issue onto the board, sets its intake fields and cuts the authoritative linked branch; plan-sprint assigns the current Iteration + Milestone + Start/Target dates, shows working-day capacity vs load and reorders the Ready queue; promote-pr opens/updates the non-closing linked PR, advances board Status across the PR lifecycle and offers a check-gated non-squash merge; intake-issues turns a raw dump into tiered, AC-bearing issues by delegating the spec body + Acceptance Criteria to spec-ops (write-spec at the tier's rigor, refine-spec for T3) and sizing/Epic-splitting from the AC-group count; sync-signals recomputes the auto Gantt-signal fields from the native blocked-by DAG and posts the project Status update. The board tracks the full branch → PR → staging → prod flow with no hand-maintenance: Status writes are idempotent + monotonic across three loosely-coupled layers, every Projects v2 write uses a GitHub App installation token (never GITHUB_TOKEN), schema edits diff before mutate to keep option/iteration IDs stable, and a skill-scoped PreToolUse guard (active only while route-issue/promote-pr run) blocks --squash and prod actions without green checks. No metered AI.
